chief_complaint: A 10-year-old male child with Schimke immuno-osseous dysplasia (SIOD) presented with respiratory symptoms after kidney transplantation.
history: The patient had a history of SIOD, characterized by T-cell immunodeficiency, and had undergone kidney transplantation. He developed early-onset Epstein-Barr virus (EBV)-associated post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) involving the lungs, which is extremely rare.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 37.5°C, blood pressure 90/60 mmHg, respiratory rate 24/min, oxygen saturation 92% on room air. Physical examination revealed bilateral lung crackles.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: WBC 12,000/μL, platelets 150,000/μL, creatinine 1.5 mg/dL. Imaging: Chest X-ray showed bilateral lung infiltrates. EBV PCR positive.
correct_diagnosis: Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) involving the lungs
differentials: Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) involving the lungs
correct_ranking: Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) involving the lungs
